chosen_action.title: Patch README and START_HERE with install + first-run commands

why_this_action.summary: The active bottleneck is conversion from interest to free use. The root README and START_HERE already sold the concept, but they did not give evaluators the shortest executable install/run path. Adding the exact prerequisite and command path is a stronger same-run conversion move than another outreach burst or another prepared-but-unshipped packet.
shared_findings_used: ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: strongest next asset is the first-task / start-here guide
marketing_workflow_audit_latest.md: same-family directory/outreach bursts should pause during the current measurement window
Ralph-Site/vendor/Ralph-Workflow/START_HERE.md: reusable install and first-run command path already proven in a shared repo artifact

expected_outcome.primary_goal: increase the share of evaluators who reach an executable first run from the Codeberg-facing repo surfaces
expected_outcome.measurement_window: review on 2026-05-31 Europe/Berlin against repo adoption deltas and follow-on funnel behavior
expected_outcome.replacement_condition: If Codeberg stars/watchers/forks are still flat and no downstream conversion evidence appears, treat this copy patch as insufficient and replace it with a stronger proof/distribution lane instead of more README-only tuning

docs_review_note.what_changed: Added a supported-agent prerequisite note plus the exact pipx install / ralph init / diagnose / run command sequence to README.md and START_HERE.md.
docs_review_note.why_here: This belongs on the top-level repo surfaces because the current bottleneck is first-run conversion, and evaluators need the executable path before they bounce deeper into docs.
docs_review_note.pruned_or_shortened: No new link farm was added; the patch fills an execution gap inside existing high-traffic sections instead of expanding navigation.
docs_review_note.duplication_reduced: Yes. The install/run path now aligns the root surfaces with the already-proven shared quickstart wording instead of leaving a mismatch between top-level marketing copy and deeper docs.
docs_review_note.why_top_level_is_better: A qualified evaluator can now move from interest to an honest first run without guessing the install command or whether Ralph replaces their agent CLI.
